items.title: KineVLA: Kinematics-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models with Bi-Level Action Decomposition

content.abstract: Introduces a kinematics-rich vision-language-action task where language commands densely encode diverse kinematic attributes throughout execution. Supports fine-grained robotic manipulation with integrated visual, linguistic, and action understanding. Represents advancement in embodied AI and robot learning from natural language.

items.title: Detecting the Machine: Comprehensive Benchmark of AI-Generated Text Detectors Across Architectures

content.abstract: Rapid proliferation of large language models creates urgent need for robust AI text detectors. Existing benchmarks evaluate narrowly. This comprehensive benchmark tests detectors across architectures, domains, and adversarial conditions. Critical for AI governance and content authenticity.

items.title: Inducing Epistemological Humility in Large Language Models: Reducing Hallucination via Targeted SFT

content.abstract: LLMs often hallucinate, producing fluent but false information, because supervised fine-tuning implicitly rewards always responding confidently. This paper introduces epistemological humility through targeted SFT to teach models to acknowledge uncertainty rather than confabulate. Fundamental advance in AI reliability.

items.title: Learning When to Attend: Conditional Memory Access for Long-Context LLMs

content.abstract: Language models struggle to generalize beyond pretraining context lengths, limiting long-horizon reasoning and retrieval. Conditional memory access mechanism enables efficient processing of contexts far exceeding training window. Addresses core scalability bottleneck of transformer architectures.

items.title: UniSAFE: Comprehensive Safety Benchmark for Unified Multimodal Models

content.abstract: Unified Multimodal Models offer powerful cross-modality capabilities but introduce new safety risks not covered by existing benchmarks. UniSAFE provides comprehensive evaluation framework across modalities. Essential infrastructure for responsible AI deployment.

items.title: When Only the Final Text Survives: Implicit Execution Tracing for Multi-Agent Attribution

content.abstract: When multi-agent AI systems produce incorrect or harmful answers, who is accountable if execution logs are unavailable? Multi-agent language systems increasingly deployed without adequate attribution mechanisms. Proposes implicit execution tracing for accountability in agentic AI.

items.title: Caging the Agents: Zero Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous AI in Healthcare

content.abstract: Autonomous AI agents with LLM capabilities including shell execution and file system access deployed in healthcare production. Proposes zero-trust security architecture constraining agent capabilities. Critical as autonomous AI enters safety-critical medical domains.

items.title: LLMs as Semantic Interface and Ethical Mediator in Neuro-Digital Ecosystems

content.abstract: Introduces Neuro-Linguistic Integration (NLI), a novel paradigm for human-technology interaction where LLMs act as semantic interface between neural cognition and digital systems. Frames LLMs as ethical mediators in brain-computer integration. Philosophical and technical implications for post-human interface design.

items.title: Is Your LLM-as-a-Recommender Agent Trustable? BiasRecBench for Algorithmic Bias Detection

content.abstract: BiasRecBench highlights vulnerability of LLM agents to systematic biases in high-value decision-making tasks including hiring, e-commerce, and content curation. Demonstrates that LLM agents inherit and amplify biases. Critical for fairness and equity in algorithmic decision-making.

items.title: An Auditable AI Agent Loop for Empirical Economics

content.abstract: AI coding agents make empirical specification search fast and cheap, but also widen hidden researcher degrees of freedom. Proposes auditable agent loop for economic research. Addresses transparency and reproducibility concerns in AI-assisted scientific research.

items.title: TRiMS: Real-Time Tracking of Minimal Sufficient Length for Efficient Reasoning via RL

content.abstract: LLMs achieve reasoning breakthroughs via long chain-of-thought sequences but this leads to severe reasoning inflation, causing excessive compute and latency. TRiMS tracks minimal sufficient reasoning length in real-time using reinforcement learning, reducing costs while maintaining quality.

items.title: ZipServ: Fast and Memory-Efficient LLM Inference with Hardware-Aware Lossless Compression

content.abstract: Lossless model compression addresses memory and bandwidth bottlenecks in LLM serving. ZipServ provides hardware-aware compression enabling larger models on existing infrastructure. Directly addresses the energy and cost constraints limiting AI deployment at scale.

items.title: The Phasor Transformer: Resolving Attention Bottlenecks on the Unit Circle

content.abstract: Novel transformer architecture that resolves dot-product self-attention quadratic token-mixing bottleneck for long-context sequences using unit circle phasor representations. Potential paradigm shift in transformer architecture design for time-series and long-context applications.

items.title: Argument Reconstruction as Supervision for Critical Thinking in LLMs

content.abstract: Human learners develop critical thinking through argument identification, reconstruction, and evaluation. This paper applies argument reconstruction as supervision signal for LLMs, teaching models to reason about argument validity. Advance toward more epistemically rigorous AI systems.

items.title: From Digital Twins to World Models: Opportunities for Mobile Edge General Intelligence

content.abstract: Rapid evolution toward 6G communication accelerates convergence of digital twins and world models at network edge. Traditional digital twins provide static replicas; world models enable dynamic, predictive simulation. Foundational for next-generation intelligent infrastructure.

items.title: Federated Computing as Code (FCaC): Sovereignty-aware Systems by Design

content.abstract: Declarative architecture enabling collaborative computation across organizations while preserving data sovereignty. Essential framework for cross-border AI systems operating under diverse regulatory regimes (EU AI Act, national data protection laws).

items.title: QuantFL: Sustainable Federated Learning for Edge IoT via Pre-Trained Model Quantisation

content.abstract: Federated Learning enables privacy-preserving intelligence on IoT devices but incurs significant carbon footprint due to high energy cost. QuantFL addresses sustainability through quantized pre-trained models, reducing environmental impact of distributed AI.

items.title: Proof-of-Authorship for Diffusion-based AI Generated Content

content.abstract: AI-generated content introduces new challenges in intellectual property protection. Proposes cryptographic proof-of-authorship framework for diffusion model outputs. Addresses ownership and authenticity in an era where AI-generated and human-created content become indistinguishable.

items.title: General Circuit Compilation Protocol into Partially Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing Architecture

content.abstract: Circuit execution protocol for the space-time efficient analog rotation (STAR) architecture using lattice surgery. Advances practical quantum computing by enabling fault-tolerant circuit compilation in near-term quantum hardware.

items.title: SCALE: Scalable Conditional Atlas-Level Endpoint Transport for Virtual Cell Models

content.abstract: Virtual cell models enable in silico experimentation predicting cellular responses to genetic, chemical, or cytokine perturbations. SCALE provides scalable transport framework connecting atlas-level cellular states. Foundational for computational biology and drug discovery.

items.title: DexEXO: Wearability-First Dexterous Exoskeleton for Scalable Robot Learning

content.abstract: Scaling dexterous robot learning constrained by difficulty of collecting high-quality demonstrations. DexEXO prioritizes wearability to enable diverse operators to generate training data. Removes critical bottleneck in robot learning from human demonstrations.

items.title: Interpreting Context-Aware Human Preferences for Multi-Objective Robot Navigation

content.abstract: Robots operating in human-shared environments must achieve navigation objectives while adapting to nuanced human preferences. Proposes interpretable framework for context-aware multi-objective navigation. Key for social robotics and human-robot cohabitation.

items.title: Efficient Soft Actor-Critic with LLM-Based Action-Level Guidance for Continuous Control

content.abstract: GuidedSAC leverages LLMs to provide action-level guidance for reinforcement learning in continuous control. Bridges language understanding and physical control. Represents convergence of foundation models and robotics control theory.

items.title: Proactive Knowledge Inquiry in Doctor-Patient Dialogue: Stateful Extraction and Path-Aware Planning

content.abstract: Most medical record pipelines remain output-oriented post-consultation. This paper proposes proactive knowledge inquiry during doctor-patient dialogue with stateful extraction and belief updating. Shifts AI from passive medical transcription to active clinical decision support.

items.title: Noise-Resilient Nonadiabatic Geometric Quantum Computation for Bosonic Binomial Codes

content.abstract: Binomial codes offer parity-mediated loss immunity and error recoverability. Geometric phases provide inherent noise resilience. Combination enables robust quantum computation. Advances toward practical error-protected quantum information processing.

items.title: Optimizing Logical Mappings for Quantum Low-Density Parity Check Codes

content.abstract: Early demonstrations of fault-tolerant quantum systems pave the way for logical-level compilation. Optimizes logical mappings for quantum LDPC codes. Practical framework for scaling quantum error correction toward utility-scale quantum computing.

items.title: Mirror Descent on Riemannian Manifolds

content.abstract: Mirror Descent scalable first-order optimization method extended to Riemannian manifolds. Applications in image processing, policy optimization, and neural network training. Fundamental mathematical advance with broad computational implications.

items.title: Language on Demand, Knowledge at Core: Composing LLMs with Translation Models for Extensible Multilinguality

content.abstract: LLMs exhibit strong general intelligence but multilingual performance remains highly imbalanced. Proposes composing LLMs with encoder-decoder translation models for extensible multilingual capability without retraining. Addresses critical language equity gap in AI.

items.title: Bringing Network Coding into Multi-Robot Systems

content.abstract: Communication is core enabler for multi-robot systems. Network coding applied to multi-robot communication for improved reliability and efficiency. Direct applications in autonomous vehicle coordination and robotic swarm operations.

items.title: GUIDE: GenAI Units In Digital Design Education

content.abstract: Open courseware repository with runnable labs for integrating generative AI into hardware design education. Addresses critical need for AI literacy in engineering curricula. Democratizes access to cutting-edge AI tools for educational institutions.
